storeOrigin: This file defines no store fields of its own. Every store handle arrives as a function parameter named `store beads.Store` (the controller's single work/Router store) and, on the close-family/sync functions, an additional `rigStores map[string]beads.Store` (per-rig work stores). All callers in city_runtime.go pass `cr.cityBeadStore()` (returns cr.cs.CityBeadStore() or cr.standaloneCityStore) as `store` and `cr.rigBeadStores()` as `rigStores`; cmd_start.go passes the same `store`/rigStores it opened. There is NO typed sessionStore in this file yet — the planned `resolveSessionStore(store, cr.cfg, cr.cityPath, cr.rec)` (cmd/gc/class_store.go:213) must be derived at the city_runtime.go entry points and threaded additively into every session/wait op below, while `store`/`rigStores` stays the WORK store for the work-assignment ops. Helper-on-store wrappers setMeta/setMetaBatch/closeBead/closeFailedCreateBead/cancelStateAssignedToRetiredSessionBead all act on whatever `store` is handed in, so the class of those calls is determined by the calling function's context (they are invoked on session beads in every case here, so they want the session store — EXCEPT when reused as the work-release path).

summary: cmd/gc/session_beads.go is the controller's session-bead reconciler and is the single highest-risk file for the Router retirement: it interleaves SESSION-class ops (type=session/gc:session lifecycle + type=gate/gc:wait waits) with WORK-class assignment reads/writes through ONE store handle (`store`) plus `rigStores`. All store handles arrive as parameters; city_runtime.go passes cr.cityBeadStore()/cr.rigBeadStores(). Pure session/wait ops (loadSessionBeads/ListAllSessionBeads, Create, alias/name guards, setMeta(Batch), Close of session beads, CancelWaits/ReassignWaits/ListSessionWaitBeads) must route to resolveSessionStore — these are benign if missed (sessions/waits leak). The LANDMINES are the work-assignment ops that filter OUT IsSessionBeadOrRepairable and read by assignee: workAssignmentStores + unclaim/reassign (633-737), sessionHasOpenAssignedWorkForConfig guards (2196/2207), and especially the work-release embedded in closeBead via releaseWorkFromClosedSessionBead (2321/2361/2387) plus the ambiguous-federated close-family (closeSessionBeadIfUnassigned / ...RuntimeStoppedAndUnassigned at 916/961/1028/1573/1587, reapStale 1916, dead-runtime cleanup 2012) — each of these MUST keep its work reads on the work store while the session-close leg moves to the session store; misrouting any of them to an (empty, when relocated) session store either drops work assignment or closes a session holding live work (mass closure). One extra caution: sweepProcessTableOrphans store.Get (2162) treats ErrNotFound as 'confirmed absent -> terminate runtime', so a misroute to an empty session store could SIGTERM a live runtime — marked landmine. extmsg.* binding ops (749/752/770) are mail/extmsg class, not session, and are already covered by the mail relocation.

session_reconciler.go is the bead-driven session lifecycle loop. Every store handle in it arrives by PARAMETER (no receiver fields; this is a package-level function file, not a method file). The two production entry points in city_runtime.go (tick: reconcileSessionBeadsTracedWithNamedDemand @2221; control-dispatcher tick: reconcileSessionBeadsAtPathWithNamedDemand @2820) both pass store=cr.cityBeadStore() (today a federating coordrouter.Router) plus rigStores=cr.rigBeadStores(); the finalize phase (@1127) passes the same store into finalizeDrainAckStopPendingSessions. So a sessionStore derived once via resolveSessionStore(store, cfg, cityPath, rec) at those three entry points must be threaded ADDITIVELY through this whole call tree alongside the existing work store.

Two distinct store-use families share the SAME store param here and are the entire landmine surface:
(1) SESSION ops — keyed on session.ID: store.SetMetadataBatch / store.SetMetadata / store.Get / closeBead / closeFailedCreateBead / healExpiredTimers / persistSessionCircuitBreakerMetadata / resetSessionCircuitBreakerState / ResolveSessionBeadByExactID. These MUST move to sessionStore. Misrouting them is benign-ish (they target session beads which live on sessionStore).
(2) WORK ops — store.List / beads.ReadyLive / GraphOnlyList/Ready, ALWAYS post-filtered by sessionpkg.IsSessionBeadOrRepairable or hasNonSessionAssignedWork to KEEP non-session assigned work. These MUST stay on the work store. Routing ANY of these to the (empty-of-work) sessionStore returns "no assigned work" → the close/drain/recycle/max-age gates fire and CLOSE LIVE SESSIONS / DROP WORK ASSIGNMENT. This is the mass-closure landmine.

The dangerous functions are the CLOSE FAMILY (closeSessionBeadIfReachableStoreUnassigned, and the inline poolFreeable close at ~2572, finalizeDrainAckStoppedSession, reconcileDrainAckStopPending, the orphan/suspended + config-drift + max-age branches): each does a WORK read (sessionHasOpenAssignedWork*) AND a SESSION close/SetMetadata through ONE store handle. When split, the work read keeps the work store and ONLY the session mutation moves to sessionStore. The helper sessionHasOpenAssignedWorkForReachableStore (and its peers) is the single chokepoint for all work reads — it takes store+rigStores and federates; it is the right place to pin the work store. Note all the List/Ready work helpers (lines ~2730-3270, 3906, 3951) operate on whatever store is handed in; their CALLERS decide the class, so they themselves are work-store-bound by contract and should never be handed sessionStore.

NUANCE — sessionAttachedForConfigDrift @3491 calls sessionpkg.ResolveSessionBeadByExactID(store, session.ID): that is a SESSION read and must move to sessionStore (today it works only because the Router federates the session bead). Easy to miss because it is buried in an attachment probe, not an obvious lifecycle write.
storeOrigin: No receiver/struct fields — this file is package-level functions. Every store handle is a PARAMETER named `store beads.Store` plus `rigStores map[string]beads.Store`. Origin is city_runtime.go: store=cr.cityBeadStore() (=cr.cs.CityBeadStore() or cr.standaloneCityStore; today a coordrouter.Router federating sessions+work+graph), rigStores=cr.rigBeadStores(). Threaded in via reconcileSessionBeadsTracedWithNamedDemand @city_runtime.go:2221, reconcileSessionBeadsAtPathWithNamedDemand @2820, and finalizeDrainAckStopPendingSessions @1127. A few helpers also take sp/cfg/cityPath used only to RESOLVE which store (reachableStoresForSession, assignedWorkStoreRefForAgent) — they do not open stores.

summary: session_wake.go contains 9 store-use sites and ZERO work-store sites — it is a pure SESSION-class file. Every direct beads op (preWakeCommit SetMetadataBatch:68, completeDrain SetMetadataBatch:611) writes incarnation/drain metadata to the session lifecycle bead (session.ID), and every indirect store use (verifiedStop:640, verifiedInterrupt:657, the two workerSessionTargetRunningWithConfig probes:473,588) resolves the session bead via IsSessionBeadOrRepairable-gated ResolveSessionBeadByExactID/ResolveSessionID to obtain a worker.Handle. None of them read or write work-assignment beads, dep beads, or demand — those reads live in session_reconciler.go (sessionHas*AssignedWork, *ReachableStore helpers), NOT here. Therefore there are NO landmine sites and NO mixed funcs in this file: threading is unambiguous — convert every `store beads.Store` parameter to the session store. The only caution is correctness-of-resolution: if the session store is empty/misrouted, the worker-handle probes fail closed (running=false) and would falsely complete a drain on a live session, and the metadata writes would no-op the wake/drain protocol — a session-management regression, but not the work-assignment mass-closure landmine. The work-store landmine surface for the drain pipeline is upstream in session_reconciler.go (the functions that call into advanceSessionDrains* AND read assigned work through the same handle), not in session_wake.go. The drain-ack/cancel family (setReconcilerDrainAckMetadata, cancel*Drain*, reconcilerDrainAckMatchesSession, etc.) touches only runtime.Provider env metadata, not beads, so it is store-agnostic and unaffected by the Router retirement.

storeOrigin: Every store handle in this file is a parameter named `store beads.Store` (never a receiver field — this file has no methods on CityRuntime). The handle is the single raw federating coordrouter.Router threaded down from two families of city_runtime.go entry points: (1) the START path: (cr *CityRuntime) reconcile tick -> city_runtime.go:2221 reconcileSessionBeadsTracedWithNamedDemand(store=cr.<workStore/Router>) -> session_reconciler.go:2599 executePlannedStartsTraced(...,store,...) which is the trampoline in THIS file at line 2110; from there `store` flows into prepareStartCandidateForCity/buildPreparedStart/commitStartResultTraced/etc. (2) the STOP/INTERRUPT path: controller.go:993/1008/1093 and cmd_restart.go:217 call stopTargetsBounded/interruptTargetsBoundedWithForceSignal(...,store,...) directly with the same Router. clk/rec/sp/cfg are also threaded as params. To relocate the SESSIONS class, a typed sessionStore derived via resolveSessionStore(store,cr.cfg,cr.cityPath,cr.rec) at those city_runtime.go entry points must be threaded ADDITIVELY: session/wait ops take sessionStore, work-assignment reads (resolveTaskWorkDir/resolveTaskOptionOverrides) and the closeBead work-release cascade keep the work store.

summary: session_lifecycle_parallel.go is the START + STOP/INTERRUPT executor of the session reconciler. Every store handle is a `store beads.Store` parameter (no receiver state) threaded from the CityRuntime reconcile tick (city_runtime.go:2221 -> session_reconciler.go:2599) for starts, and from controller.go/cmd_restart.go for stops/interrupts — today a single federating coordrouter.Router. The overwhelming majority of store uses are SESSION-class: session-bead Get/SetMetadata/List/Close and worker-boundary observe/peek/stop/interrupt/kill, all keyed on the session bead (Type=session / gc:session) — these must move to the session store derived via resolveSessionStore. TWO classes of WORK-class reads MUST stay on the work store: (1) resolveTaskWorkDir@853 and resolveTaskOptionOverrides@841 inside buildPreparedStartWithWorkDirResolver — store.List{Assignee,Status:in_progress,Live,TierBoth} over WORK beads assigned to the session, reading work_dir/opt_* metadata; misrouting them to an empty session store silently drops worktree/option overrides on a live city. (3) the CLOSE-FAMILY landmine: rollbackPendingCreate@2065 / rollbackPendingCreateClearingClaim@2081 call closeBead/closeFailedCreateBead, which (session_beads.go:2282) close the SESSION bead AND cascade releaseWorkFromClosedSessionBead — a WORK read+write that clears assignee and resets in_progress->open. The session close must target the session store while the cascaded work-release must target the work store; running the whole cascade against an empty session store would fail to release work assignment (dropped work) — this is the mass-closure/work-drop surface. No ambiguous-federated post-filter List remains in THIS file (loadSessionBeads/loadSessionBeadSnapshot already filter to session beads via IsSessionBeadOrRepairable, so they are cleanly session-only). The threading priority is the six mixed functions, chiefly buildPreparedStartWithWorkDirResolver and the closeBead-rooted rollback path, which must receive BOTH stores.

storeOrigin: Both store handles are PARAMETERS, not receiver fields — this file has no receiver methods. Every site receives `store beads.Store` (today the federating coordrouter.Router work handle) plus `rigStores map[string]beads.Store` (per-rig WORK stores) from its caller. The `session beads.Bead` passed in is ALWAYS a session-class bead (type "session"/label "gc:session"): it is the subject of the close decision. So a single `store` param is used here for BOTH a work-assignment read (the guard check) and a session-bead Close (the close-family). closeSessionBeadIfReachableStoreUnassigned additionally takes `cityPath` + `cfg` used downstream to compute reachable WORK stores. When the Router is retired, the caller must thread a typed sessionStore additively so the close-family targets it while the guard read stays on the work store/rigStores.

summary: session_work_guard.go has exactly two free functions and six store-use sites. The pattern is uniform and dangerous: each function first does a WORK-class assigned-work read (line 36 sessionHasOpenAssignedWorkForConfig; line 67 sessionHasOpenAssignedWorkForReachableStore) — both LANDMINE=true, klass=work, must stay on the work/rig/graph store — and THEN, only if no work is assigned, does a SESSION-class close of the passed-in session bead (closeFailedCreateBead at L45/L76, closeBead at L47/L78) which belongs on the relocated session store. Both functions are therefore MIXED: they route a work read and a session close through the SAME `store` parameter. The close-family helper closeBead is itself doubly mixed (session Get/Close + work releaseWorkFromClosedSessionBead). Threading rule for retiring the Router: add a typed sessionStore (derived once at beadReconcileTick / controlDispatcherTick via resolveSessionStore) and pass it so the close-family hits the session store, while leaving the assigned-work guard read on the work store + rigStores (and the GraphOnlyListFor capability probe on the work/graph handle). Misrouting either work read to the empty session store closes live sessions / drops work — production outage; misrouting a session close to the work store is only a benign leak. No nudge/mail/order/pure-graph ops appear in this file.

storeOrigin: All store handles in this file originate from CALLER PARAMETERS, never opened here. (1) `store beads.Store` param to buildDesiredState / buildDesiredStateWithSessionBeads / refreshDesiredStateWithSessionBeads — at the live entry point this is `cr.cityBeadStore()` (the federating coordrouter.Router) threaded from CityRuntime.buildDesiredState (city_runtime.go:2973) and from loadDemandSnapshot/refresh paths. (2) `rigStores map[string]beads.Store` param = `cr.rigBeadStores()` — per-rig work stores; these are pure WORK stores and never hold city session beads. (3) `bp.beadStore` is set to the same `store` param inside newAgentBuildParams (agent_build_params.go:112), so every bp.beadStore.* site is the SAME federating handle as `store`. (4) `defaultScaleCheckTarget.store` and the local `activeStore.store`/`workStore.store` structs are just re-packaged copies of `store`+`rigStores`. (5) The aligned `AssignedWorkStores []beads.Store` returned to callers are the per-bead WORK stores captured during collection. Threading plan: a typed sessionStore (resolveSessionStore(store, cfg, cityPath, rec)) must be threaded ADDITIVELY into buildDesiredStateWithSessionBeads and into agentBuildParams (a new bp.sessionStore field) so the SESSION sites below read/write it while the work sites keep using `store`/`rigStores`/`bp.beadStore`.

summary: build_desired_state.go is the controller's desired-state builder and is the single largest mixed session/work surface in the file. Store handles are NEVER opened here — they arrive as the `store` param (= cr.cityBeadStore(), the federating Router today), `rigStores` (work stores), and bp.beadStore (= same `store`). SESSION-class sites (must move to a typed sessionStore, all landmine=false / benign-leak): the session snapshot loads loadSessionBeadSnapshot (430, 1975) and collectAllOpenSessionBeads/session.ListAllSessionBeads (467, 961) [type=session/gc:session, IsSessionBeadOrRepairable]; and all pool/dependency SESSION-bead writes+creates+alias checks in normalizeNonExpandingPoolSessionBead (2588/2597), recordDeferredNonExpandingPoolAliasConflict (3264), createPoolSessionBeadWithGuardedAlias (3320/3330/3338/3343/3353), and selectOrCreateDependencyPoolSessionBead (3742/3751). WORK-class sites (MUST stay on work store, landmine=true — the mass-closure surface): collectAssignedWorkBeadsWithStores and its List(in_progress) 1113 / List(open) 1131 / Ready 1191,1197 passes; defaultScaleCheckCounts Ready 1466; defaultNamedSessionDemand Ready 1535; collectOpenUnassignedRoutedWork List 3707; the shared helpers listBothTiersForControllerDemand 1703 and (live)readyForControllerDemandQuery 1717/1763; and the work-bead WRITERS stampRunSessionIdentity 3482 + stampRunRootFromStep 3508/3525, canonicalizeLegacyBoundAssignedWork 3608, canonicalizeLegacyBoundUnassignedRoutedWork 3669. Top threading priority + landmine concentration is buildDesiredStateWithSessionBeads itself, which derives both classes from one handle, plus the three mixed writer functions (stampRunSessionIdentity, canonicalizeLegacyBoundAssignedWork) that read the session snapshot for guards but write work beads. No truly ambiguous-federated List remained in THIS file: appendWorkUnique always filters Type==sessionBeadType out of every work List, so the work passes are cleanly work-only and the session passes are cleanly session-only — the only federation is the cached+live merge inside readyForControllerDemandQuery (work store, not cross-class). Derive sessionStore once at city_runtime.go entry points and thread it additively into buildDesiredStateWithSessionBeads and agentBuildParams; leave store/rigStores/bp.beadStore on the work paths untouched.

storeOrigin: Two origins. (1) CLI command paths open the store locally: openCityStore(stderr, name) (main.go:1080) and openCityStoreAt(cityPath) (main.go:1107) return a raw beads.Store that today is the federating coordrouter.Router. Used by cmdSessionWait (L193), cmdWaitSetStateResult (L642), doWaitListFallback (L393), doWaitInspectFallback (L508). (2) Controller paths receive the store as a `store beads.Store` parameter threaded down from cmd/gc/city_runtime.go beadReconcileTick (L2089, store := cr.cityBeadStore()) into prepareWaitWakeStateForCityWithSnapshot (city_runtime.go:2197) and dispatchReadyWaitNudgesWithSnapshot (city_runtime.go:2251). In the controller paths a SECOND handle `nudgeStore` is already passed alongside, derived at the entry point via resolveNudgesStore(store, cr.cfg, cr.cityPath, cr.rec) — the same additive-threading pattern the sessionStore will follow. cmdWaitSetStateResult derives nudgeStore inline (L651-655) via resolveNudgesStore. Wait-list/inspect API paths use api.Client (apiClient via waitListAPIClient/waitInspectAPIClient) and never touch a beads.Store directly. Nudge withdrawal uses openNudgesClassStore(cityPath) (L1453, already relocated nudge store). Dep-bead resolution opens per-convoy-scope work stores via openStoreAtForCity / convoyStoreCandidates (loadWaitDependencyBead L920-957).
summary: cmd_wait.go is the durable session-wait (type "gate"/label "gc:wait") lifecycle surface. Almost every store.Get/Create/List/SetMetadata/Close on wait beads and session beads is SESSION class and must move to the relocated session store. The load-bearing landmine is loadWaitDependencyBead and depsWaitReadyDetailed*: they resolve the wait's DEPENDENCY beads (real work/issue/convoy beads) across per-convoy work stores — those reads MUST stay on the work store; routing them at the (empty) session store would make every dep resolve as ErrNotFound, failing waits as deps-not-found and (via prepareWaitWakeState's ErrNotFound->setWaitTerminalState failed branch) terminating live waits. The two controller-threaded funcs (prepareWaitWakeStateForCityWithSnapshot, dispatchReadyWaitNudgesWithSnapshot) are mixed: they read/write session+wait beads (session store) AND call depsWaitReady (work store) AND findQueuedNudgeBead/enqueue (nudge store) through the SAME `store` handle — these are the threading priority. nudgeStore is already split out; sessionStore must be threaded the same additive way. Wait-list/inspect have API + store-fallback dual paths; the fallback store.List(label=gc:wait)/store.Get are session class.
mixedFuncs: cmdSessionWait (L184): SESSION ops (store.Get session bead L228, store.Create wait bead L256, SetMetadataBatch on wait L283 and on session wait_hold L294, setWaitTerminalState L272) PLUS WORK op (loadWaitDependencyBead L234 and depsWaitReadyDetailedForCity L270 -> dep beads on work store). One `store` handle does both. Landmine: dep loads must stay on work store.

Not a work landmine but a 3-store function.

storeOrigin: All store handles in this file derive from ONE local variable `store` obtained at line 46 via `openCityStore(stderr, "gc session wake")` (cmd/gc/main.go:1080), which returns a raw beads.Store — today a federating coordrouter.Router. This is a CLI entry-point file (cmdSessionWake), NOT city_runtime.go; there is no CityRuntime receiver and no second `workStore` handle. The single `store` is passed down into session helpers (resolveSessionIDMaterializingNamed, session.RepairEmptyType, session.WakeSession). To make this command class-aware, a typed sessionStore must be derived at this CLI entry point (analogous to resolveSessionStore at city_runtime.go entry points) and threaded in place of `store` for every site below — there are NO work-assignment reads in this file, so the entire file's store usage is session-class.

sites.beadEvidence: Opener: returns the single raw beads.Store handle used for the entire wake command. Every downstream op in this file (resolve, Get, IsSessionBeadOrRepairable, RepairEmptyType, WakeSession, SetMetadataBatch) operates only on the session bead identified by args[0] and its session-wait children. This is the origin point that must be replaced by a derived sessionStore.

mixedFuncs: cmdSessionWake — does multiple session-class ops (resolve+materialize, Get, RepairEmptyType, WakeSession which internally lists+closes session-wait beads, SetMetadataBatch) but does NOT do any work-assignment op. It is NOT a true mixed (session+work) function. It is single-class (session-only). Listed here for completeness because WakeSession bundles two distinct session sub-ops (wait close-family + session-bead update) through one handle — relevant to threading but NOT a work-misroute landmine surface. session.WakeSession (internal/session/waits.go:203) similarly bundles session-wait List/CloseAll with session-bead SetMetadataBatch through one `store` param — again all ClassSessions, no work.
callerChains: This is a CLI entry point, NOT reached from city_runtime.go. Chain: newSessionWakeCmd (cobra command, cmd/gc/cmd_session_wake.go:16) -> RunE -> cmdSessionWake (line 44) -> store = openCityStore (line 46) -> {resolveSessionIDMaterializingNamed | store.Get | RepairEmptyType | WakeSession | SetMetadataBatch}.

The reconciler-side analog: after cmdSessionWake clears wake blockers, the controller's reconcile tick (city_runtime.go) is poked via pokeController(cityPath) (line 100) and will start the woken session on its next tick — but that path runs in the controller process and derives its own store; it does NOT flow through this file's `store`. withdrawQueuedWaitNudges(cityPath, nudgeIDs) (line 96) and pokeController(cityPath) (line 100) take cityPath/nudgeIDs only, NOT a store handle, so they introduce no additional store-use site in this file.

summary: cmd/gc/cmd_session_wake.go is a single-class, session-only CLI command file. All six store-use sites operate on the one session bead identified by the wake argument and its durable session-WAIT children (type session/label gc:session and type gate/label gc:wait — both coordclass.ClassSessions). There are ZERO work-assignment reads/writes in this file, so there is NO mass-closure landmine here: every site is klass=session, landmine=false. The store originates from a single openCityStore() handle at line 46 (no separate workStore). To make the command class-aware, derive a typed sessionStore at the CLI entry (line 46) and thread it to all sites (56 resolve+materialize, 62 Get, 72 RepairEmptyType, 73 WakeSession, 83 SetMetadataBatch). The one subtlety is line 73 session.WakeSession, which internally bundles a session-wait List+CloseAll close-family with a session-bead SetMetadataBatch through one handle — but both halves are session-class, so routing the whole call to the session store is correct and drops no work. withdrawQueuedWaitNudges and pokeController take cityPath only and add no store sites.

storeOrigin:
This file does not receive a controller-threaded store. Every store handle is opened locally from a cityPath via two package-var openers (both test seams):

- openNudgeBeadStore(cityPath) (defined in nudge_beads.go:44) returns the RAW work store (openCityStoreAt -> today a coordrouter.Router). Its doc comment (nudge_beads.go:40-43) is explicit: "used as a general store across the nudge subsystem — including session reads (resolveNudgeTarget) and worker observation — so it cannot be relocated wholesale." This is the `store`/`workStore` handle threaded through nearly every func here (params named `store`, `workStore`).
- openNudgesClassStore(cityPath) (nudge_beads.go:64) opens the relocated NUDGE-SHADOW class store (resolveNudgesStore -> ClassNudges), falling back to the work store. Used only by the self-opening leaf queue helpers (claim/list/ack/release/enqueue/record/prune).
- resolveNudgesStore(workStore,cfg,cityPath,rec) (class_store.go:201) derives the nudge-class store from a work-store handle; threaded as the `nudgeStore` param.

There is NO sessionStore here yet. To retire the Router for sessions, a sessionStore = resolveSessionStore(openNudgeBeadStore(cityPath), cfg, cityPath, rec) (class_store.go:213) must be derived where these funcs open openNudgeBeadStore, and threaded to the SESSION/WAIT sites below; the WORK/nudge-shadow uses of the same handle must stay on the work store.
summary: cmd_nudge.go is a CLI/sidecar file with no controller-threaded store; it self-opens the work store (openNudgeBeadStore) and the relocated nudge-shadow store (openNudgesClassStore/resolveNudgesStore) per call. The session-class landmines are concentrated in three spots that read SESSION/WAIT beads through the work-store handle: resolveNudgeTarget (store.Get session bead + resolveSessionIDMaterializingNamed), requestManagedNudgeWake (session.WakeSession reads the session bead and cancels durable waits), and the queued-nudge delivery validators splitQueuedNudgesForDelivery/blockedQueuedNudgeReason (store.Get a durable wait bead + session.IsWaitBead). loadSessionBeads/withNudgeTargetFence also list session beads off the work handle. Everything else is nudge-shadow leaf ops (already routed to the nudges class store, benign) or worker-observation reads. The dangerous misroute would be sending the queued-delivery validator's wait-bead Get or the WakeSession session read to an empty work store (deps-not-found -> wait failed/blocked, or live session never woken).

tryDeliverQueuedNudgesByPoller — lazily opens ONE work store (line 1205) and uses it for the SESSION/WAIT validator (1222), the worker handle (1241, work/runtime), AND a SESSION stamp (1273). The poller-path twin of the drain landmine.

deliverSessionNudgeWithWorker — single `store` reaches a SESSION wake (queueManagedSessionNudgeWake -> WakeSession, 714) AND worker observation/handle (734,743). Carries both classes through one handle.

queueManagedSessionNudgeWake / enqueueManagedNudgeThenWake — pass ONE `store` to requestManagedNudgeWake (SESSION: WakeSession) while nudgeStore handles the nudge-class enqueue. The session vs nudge split is already partly done (nudgeStore param) but the session leg still rides the work handle.

sendMailNotifyWithWorker — single `store` does worker observation (1029), a SESSION stamp (1044), AND a SESSION managed-wake (enqueueManagedNudgeThenWake, 1051). Mixed session+work on the mail-notify path.

requestManagedNudgeWake — the convergence point: store.Get(sessionID) (851) + session.WakeSession (855). Pure SESSION op riding the work handle; every managed-wake caller funnels here. Make this take the session store.
stampLastNudgeDeliveredAt — pure SESSION SetMetadata (1283) called with the work store by 4 sites. Change its store param to the session store and update all callers.
withNudgeTargetFence — lists SESSION beads (loadSessionBeads, 1366) off the passed `store` to fill the fence; pure session read on a work handle.
resolveNudgeTarget — opens the work store (1079) but uses it purely for SESSION resolution (1081) + SESSION-bead Get (1083). Effectively a session-only consumer mislabeled as work.
callerChains: These are CLI/sidecar entry points, NOT city_runtime.go controller ticks. cmd nudge status -> cmdNudgeStatus -> listQueuedNudgesForTarget (nudge-class only; no session store needed).

cmd nudge drain (runtime UserPromptSubmit hook) -> cmdNudgeDrainWithFormat -> openNudgeBeadStore(work) + splitQueuedNudgesForDelivery->blockedQueuedNudgeReason->store.Get (SESSION/WAIT) + stampLastNudgeDeliveredAt (SESSION). sessionStore must be derived in cmdNudgeDrainWithFormat (right where openNudgeBeadStore is called, line 452) via resolveSessionStore(workStore,target.cfg,target.cityPath,nil).

cmd nudge poll (per-session sidecar) -> cmdNudgePoll -> openNudgeBeadStore(work, line 623) -> tryDeliverQueuedNudgesByPoller -> splitQueuedNudgesForDelivery (SESSION/WAIT) + stampLastNudgeDeliveredAt (SESSION). Derive sessionStore in cmdNudgePoll and thread it into tryDeliverQueuedNudgesByPoller alongside workStore.

gc session nudge (deliverSessionNudge, called from session-nudge command/API path) -> deliverSessionNudgeWithWorker -> queueManagedSessionNudgeWake -> enqueueManagedNudgeThenWake -> requestManagedNudgeWake -> store.Get + session.WakeSession (SESSION). Derive sessionStore in deliverSessionNudge (line 695) and thread to the wake leg.

gc mail notify (sendMailNotify) -> sendMailNotifyWithWorker -> enqueueManagedNudgeThenWake -> requestManagedNudgeWake -> WakeSession (SESSION) + stampLastNudgeDeliveredAt (SESSION). Derive sessionStore in sendMailNotify (line 1014).

resolveNudgeTarget is the shared front door for status/drain/poll/nudge/mail: it opens the work store (1079) and does the session resolve+Get. Threading the session store here (1079) feeds every downstream nudgeTarget; this is the single best place to introduce sessionStore for session resolution, though the per-entry-point funcs still each need a session store for their wake/stamp/wait legs.

NOTE: the controller's supervisor-hosted nudge dispatcher (daemon.nudge_dispatcher=supervisor) is the city_runtime.go path that exercises the SAME helpers (requestManagedNudgeWake, splitQueuedNudgesForDelivery, stampLastNudgeDeliveredAt) via the dispatcher, not via these cmd entry points — so deriving the session store at the dispatcher's call site (outside this file) is required in addition to the CLI entry points. Best-effort: the dispatcher's session-store origin is in internal/* / city_runtime.go, not visible in cmd_nudge.go.

summary: city_runtime.go threads ONE store handle, cr.cityBeadStore() (Router today; cr.cs.CityBeadStore() or cr.standaloneCityStore), into every reconciler phase. SESSION-class reads/closes (loadSessionBeadSnapshot -> ListAllSessionBeads type=session/gc:session; prepareWaitWakeState/dispatchReadyWaitNudges over gc:wait gate beads; GCSweep/reconcileSessionBeads; the reaper family) must move to a sessionStore derived once via resolveSessionStore(cr.cityBeadStore(), cr.cfg, cr.cityPath, cr.rec). WORK-class reads (releaseOrphanedPoolAssignmentsWhenSnapshotsComplete and everything downstream of closeBead's releaseWorkFromClosedSessionBead -> store.List(Assignee)/store.Update) MUST stay on the work store -- misrouting them to a relocated (empty) session store is the MASS-CLOSURE LANDMINE. The dangerous reality: the reaper funcs (cleanupDeadRuntimeSessionCorpses, reapStaleSessionBeads, reapRuntimesBoundToClosedBeads, sweepUndesiredPoolSessionBeads->GCSweepSessionBeads, beadReconcileTick) pass ONE store that downstream closeBead uses for BOTH the session close AND the work release; threading sessionStore additively here requires the close-family to take two stores (session store for close, work store for release). nudge/mail/order/extmsg/graph/wisp ops are already class-routed or graph-resident and are listed for completeness. The threading priority is beadReconcileTick (line 2089) and the run()/tick() reaper sequences (lines 539-580, 1075-1216) where sessionStore gets derived and passed down.
storeOrigin: Every store handle in this file resolves to cr.cityBeadStore() (method at L2925): returns cr.cs.CityBeadStore() when controllerState present, else cr.standaloneCityStore. The standalone path is set in run() (L394-397 openCityStoreAt) and reloadConfigTraced (L1975-1981). Rig stores come from cr.rigBeadStores() (L2932): cr.cs.BeadStores() minus the city, else cr.standaloneRigStores (built by buildStandaloneRigStores L3130 / openStoreAtForCity, set L399 + L1982). Order-tracking sweeps open throwaway stores via newCityRuntimeOpenSweepStore (=openStoreAtForCity, L38) at L264, L1542. Per-class seams already exist: resolveNudgesStore (L1517,2197,2251,2760), resolveClassStore(...Messaging) (L1518). resolveSessionStore is NOT yet called anywhere in this file -- that is exactly the gap this plan fills: it must be derived from cr.cityBeadStore() at the entry points and threaded additively alongside the work store.
